artifacts.name: Hip jewelry, with money
artifacts.legend: Married women wore it all the time. During weddings, groom's attendants wore it on their right hip.

quiz.question: Who traditionally wore the hip jewelry with money?

artifacts.name: Bag
artifacts.legend: Lunch bag, worn on the belt when going to work.

quiz.question: What was the main use of the bag made from inner bark?

artifacts.name: Beer bucket
artifacts.legend: For drawing beer. Used mainly in sacrifice.

quiz.question: What was the beer bucket mainly used for?

artifacts.name: Hunting kit
artifacts.materials: Linen (cord), leather, and horn, birch (cork), and metal
artifacts.legend: They belonged to the seller's grandfather.

quiz.question: Who originally owned the hunting kit?

artifacts.name: Robe
artifacts.legend: Worn over a shirt and apron, both in summer and winter (headscarves were worn - factory product).

quiz.question: When was the robe typically worn?
options: D. In both summer and winter

final_quiz.question: What was the hunting kit made of?
options: C. Linen (cord), leather, and horn, birch (cork), metal
